title: clematis montana var. glabrescens
intro: Clematis montana var. glabrescens is a deciduous climber that is native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia, characterized by its white, fragrant flowers and woody stems. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Clematis montana var. glabrescens are said to include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots are typically dried and used in infusions or decoctions to extract its active compounds, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
